titleEn: Conference on Teaching Innovation in Economics for Secondary and University Education
excerptEn: On 9 April 2016, the Boardroom of the Faculty of Economics and Business of the University of Salamanca will host the Conference on Teaching Innovation in Economics, with the aim of creating a forum in which colleagues from secondary and university education can share and learn from…
contentEn:
On 9 April 2016, the Boardroom of the Faculty of Economics and Business of the University of Salamanca will host the Conference on Teaching Innovation in Economics, with the aim of creating a forum in which colleagues from secondary and university education can share and learn from the innovative experiences carried out in the economics classroom.

The conference is free of charge; to register, you only need to send your full name and national identity document (DNI) number to jornadasfeye@usal.es before 5 April.

Detailed information about the conference is available here.

titleEn: Start of the Initial Training Plan for University Teaching Staff
excerptEn: On the morning of Friday 26 February, the opening session of the Initial Training for University Teaching Staff course took place. This activity marks the start of the set of activities that make up the General Training Plan for teaching and research staff (PDI) for this academic year. The training actions are aimed at all the teaching and research staff of…
contentEn:
On the morning of Friday 26 February, the opening session of the Initial Training for University Teaching Staff course took place. This activity marks the start of the set of activities that make up the General Training Plan for teaching and research staff (PDI) for this academic year. The training actions are aimed at all the teaching and research staff of the University of Salamanca and will run mainly during the second half of May and throughout the month of June.

The activity that opens the Programme brings together a group of early-career lecturers, along with their mentors, who will receive special training tailored to the needs of new teaching staff. The photograph shows early-career lecturers, some of the mentors and the course coordinators, María José Rodríguez Conde and Susana Olmos Migueláñez, together with the Vice-Rector for Promotion and Coordination, José Ángel Domínguez, who opened the session and gave a lecture on “Teaching activity and the professional career of university teaching staff”.

titleEn: Training for Ibero-American University Teaching Staff at the IUCE
excerptEn: Today, 18 January, the specialisation course in University Teaching Methodology was opened by the Director of the General Foundation, Oscar González Benito, and by the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences, María José Rodríguez Conde, as its coordinator. It will run from 18 to 22…
contentEn:
Today, 18 January, the specialisation course in University Teaching Methodology was opened by the Director of the General Foundation, Oscar González Benito, and by the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences, María José Rodríguez Conde, as its coordinator.

It will run from 18 to 22 January, with a duration of 40 hours, in room 17 of the IUCE, and will be attended by 25 lecturers from the Universidad Libre de Colombia. The speakers are lecturers from the Educational Evaluation and Guidance Group and the Educational Technology Research Group.

titleEn: Training for non-university teaching staff within the Regional Government of Castilla y León's School of Researchers innovation project
excerptEn: On 3 and 4 December, the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca and the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP) organised the initial training session of the educational innovation project “School of Researchers Programme”, now in its third edition. This activity, which…
contentEn:
On 3 and 4 December, the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca and the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP) organised the initial training session of the educational innovation project “School of Researchers Programme”, now in its third edition.

This activity, which will be attended by 30 Pre-school, Primary and Secondary Education teachers from the Salamanca district, is based on the RESOLUTION of 23 October 2014, of the Directorate-General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, establishing the educational innovation project «School of Researchers Programme» for experimenting with new methodologies of continuing teacher training in the field of educational research and innovation during the 2014-15 school year. (BOCyL of 3 November 2014).

This programme has launched the second School of Researchers through three training phases. A face-to-face, theoretical phase, which serves as a source of knowledge and an introduction to research, as well as the starting point for the project. In it, different research initiation groups will be set up to address a range of educational problems. An online collaborative phase, which will take place through the CSFP's virtual classroom and will consist of exchange and group learning under the coordination of a member of the IUCE research team. And finally, a practical phase of experimentation, learning and hands-on application in the classroom, to test the link between research and teacher training and teachers' professional development.

The Educational Evaluation and Guidance Group, within the GRIAL group, coordinated by María José Rodríguez Conde, is responsible for monitoring this activity.

titleEn: Closing of the RED-U 2015 Conference
Follow the RED-U Conference via live stream
RED-U 2015 Conference
excerptEn: On 26 and 27 November 2015, at the Hospedería Fonseca, the University of Salamanca together with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) will host the RED-U 2015 conference, whose theme will be «From «amateur» teaching to research-based teaching: emerging academic roles». The objectives that RED-U…
contentEn:
On 26 and 27 November 2015, at the Hospedería Fonseca, the University of Salamanca together with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) will host the RED-U 2015 conference, whose theme will be «From «amateur» teaching to research-based teaching: emerging academic roles».

The objectives that RED-U has set for this conference are:

  1. To analyse collectively the meaning of these new emerging academic roles
  2. To become aware of how this academic conception of teaching is taking shape worldwide, and in particular at European level
  3. To learn about the sector-specific realities of disciplines that already show a significant level of development in the academic treatment of teaching
  4. To create the conditions for connecting the different academic teaching networks of the various disciplines and to stimulate unprecedented initiatives in this field

Registration, which costs €125 for RED-U members and €175 for everyone else, is completed through the conference website, http://jornadas2015.red-u.org/inscripcion.

In addition, all the information on the speakers, as well as the programme, can be found at http://jornadas2015.red-u.org.

titleEn: TEEM'15 Conference
excerptEn: This year the international conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM’15 – http://teemconference.eu) will take place in Porto (Portugal) on 7-9 October 2015 at the Polytechnic of Porto – School of Engineering (ISEP). This edition will maintain its multicultural character, combining different aspects of technology applied to the development of the Knowledge…
contentEn:
This year the international conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM’15 – http://teemconference.eu) will take place in Porto (Portugal) on 7-9 October 2015 at the Polytechnic of Porto – School of Engineering (ISEP).

This edition will maintain its multicultural character, combining different aspects of technology applied to the development of the Knowledge Society, with special attention to educational and learning topics relating to, but not limited to, the following broad-ranging research areas:

  • Education Assessment and Orientation
  • Human-Computer Interaction
  • eLearning
  • Computers in Education
  • Communication Media and Education
  • Medicine and Education
  • Robotics in Education
  • Engineering and Education
  • Information Society and Education

In addition to the various scheduled sessions, the third edition of TEEM will host the 6th International Workshop on Software Engineering for E-learning (ISELEAR’15).

titleEn: The 2015 Initial Teacher Training (FIPU) course comes to an end
excerptEn: On Wednesday 24 September, the Aula de los Angelitos of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) hosted the final session of the 2015 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff, aimed at early-career lecturers, associate lecturers and grant holders with assigned teaching duties, with the aim of: Introducing early-career lecturers to…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 24 September, the Aula de los Angelitos of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) hosted the final session of the 2015 Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff, aimed at early-career lecturers, associate lecturers and grant holders with assigned teaching duties, with the aim of:

  • Introducing early-career lecturers to the activities of planning, delivering and assessing university teaching.
  • Developing reflective thinking in the course of their teaching practice.
  • Planning and applying methodologies and assessment in an efficient and coherent way.
  • Using the various forms of pedagogical communication effectively and coherently.

titleEn: Visit to the IUCE by lecturers from several Brazilian universities
excerptEn: Several lecturers from different Brazilian universities who are taking an international Spanish course are visiting the IUCE today, Thursday 16 July, in order to make initial contact with the research groups active at the Institute. Their interest was prompted by a desire to learn about its Doctoral Programme in Education in the Knowledge…
contentEn:
Several lecturers from different Brazilian universities who are taking an international Spanish course are visiting the IUCE today, Thursday 16 July, in order to make initial contact with the research groups active at the Institute. Their interest was prompted by a desire to learn about its Doctoral Programme in Education in the Knowledge Society and the journal published by the Institute, Education in the Knowledge Society (EKS). María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the IUCE, and Francisco J. García Peñalvo, coordinator of the Doctoral Programme and editor of the EKS Journal, welcomed them.

The visiting lecturers were:

  • Professor Maria Cristina Lima Paniago. Education. Universidade Católica Dom Bosco. PhD in Applied Linguistics. Leader of a study and research group on Educational Technology and Distance Education (GETED). Member of the editorial board of the journal «Serie Estudos» http://www.serie-estudos.ucdb.br/index.php/serie-estudos.
  • Professor Maria de Fatima Nascimento do Amaral. Nursing. Universidade do Grande Rio-UNIGRANRIO.
  • Professor Marcello Batista Ribeiro. Computer Science. Master's degree in Regional Development and the Environment. Universidade Federal de Rondônia.

titleEn: Management of Educational Innovation
excerptEn: On 6 and 7 July, the IUCE hosted the course Management of Educational Innovation, part of the University of Salamanca's 2015 Teaching Training Plan. Ángel Fidalgo Blanco, Director of the Information Technology Innovation Laboratory at the Technical University of Madrid, and María Luisa Sein…
contentEn: On 6 and 7 July, the IUCE hosted the course Management of Educational Innovation, part of the University of Salamanca's 2015 Teaching Training Plan. Ángel Fidalgo Blanco, Director of the Information Technology Innovation Laboratory at the Technical University of Madrid, and María Luisa Sein Echaluce Lacleta, Director of the Virtual Campus of the University of Zaragoza, delivered this 8-hour course, which is linked to the University of Salamanca teaching innovation project ID2014/0312, «Implementation of a comprehensive knowledge management system for the teaching innovation processes of the University of Salamanca».

titleEn: Juan José Mena takes part in a European KA 1 project with Turkey
excerptEn: Juan José Mena, a researcher in the GITE group (Research Group on Innovation in Educational Technology), is taking part in the project «Using ICT (Information and Communication Technologies) tools in math, science and english courses» (2014-1-TR01-KA101-004923), funded by the Erasmus+ School Staff Mobility (KA 1) sub-programme within the European Union's Lifelong Learning Programme. The project,…
contentEn:
Juan José Mena, a researcher in the GITE group (Research Group on Innovation in Educational Technology), is taking part in the project «Using ICT (Information and Communication Technologies) tools in math, science and english courses» (2014-1-TR01-KA101-004923), funded by the Erasmus+ School Staff Mobility (KA 1) sub-programme within the European Union's Lifelong Learning Programme.

The project, which runs from August 2014 to July 2016 with funding of €25,700, is coordinated by Atíke Akel Secondary School (Turkey) and involves ITC International (Czech Republic), the University of Salamanca (Spain), Saint Ninian’s Primary School (Scotland) and Stadtteilschule Arheilgen (Germany).

Throughout this week, the University of Salamanca, the Faculty of Education and the University Institute of Education Sciences have hosted Turkish and German teachers as part of the project's activities.

titleEn: Thesis defence of Martina Marcela Martínez Preciado
excerptEn: Martina Marcela Martínez Preciado, lecturer in Radio Production and Multimedia Production at the University of Sonora (Mexico), defends today, 15 June 2015, at the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca, her doctoral thesis entitled «Moderation via chat and its relationship with participation and knowledge construction. Application…
contentEn:
Martina Marcela Martínez Preciado, lecturer in Radio Production and Multimedia Production at the University of Sonora (Mexico), defends today, 15 June 2015, at the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca, her doctoral thesis entitled «Moderation via chat and its relationship with participation and knowledge construction. Application and analysis in the region of Sonora, Mexico», supervised by María José Rodríguez Conde and Joaquín García Carrasco.

Part of the doctoral thesis was developed during the 2013/2014 academic year, in a research stay carried out by the doctoral candidate at the University Institute of Education Sciences as part of the Educational Evaluation and Guidance Group (GE2O).

titleEn: Juan José Igartua, seminar in the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme
excerptEn: On 10 and 11 June 2015, Professor Juan José Igartua, Director of the Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) of the University of Salamanca (http://www.ocausal.es), taught the course “Mediation, moderation and moderated mediation analysis with bootstrapping techniques. An introduction to Andrew F. Hayes' PROCESS macro” (lasting…
contentEn: On 10 and 11 June 2015, Professor Juan José Igartua, Director of the Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) of the University of Salamanca (http://www.ocausal.es), taught the course “Mediation, moderation and moderated mediation analysis with bootstrapping techniques. An introduction to Andrew F. Hayes' PROCESS macro” (lasting 10 hours) as a methodological seminar in the Education in the Knowledge Society Doctoral Programme of the University of Salamanca. Mediation and moderation are two statistical concepts of great relevance to scientific progress in any discipline, and recent developments, thanks to Professor Hayes' contribution, have brought about a genuine revolution and made the task of testing mediation and moderation processes far more accessible with the creation of the PROCESS macro (implemented from the SPSS program), which is based on bootstrapping techniques. Why is mediation analysis important? Because it makes it possible to test (theoretical) explanations that help us understand why a given (independent) variable influences another (dependent variable), and through which processes or mechanisms. What is a moderator variable? It is one that modifies the relationship between an independent variable and a dependent variable, so that the sign and strength of the relationship between the two variables depend on the influence of the moderator variable. It therefore answers questions such as when, or under which conditions, contexts or types of people, a causal relationship between two variables occurs. Finally, the analysis of “moderated mediation” belongs to the category of what Hayes (2013) calls “conditional process analysis” and combines mediation (process) analysis with moderation analysis.

titleEn: School of Researchers day in Burgos
excerptEn: On 4 June, the Faculty of Education in Burgos hosted the final face-to-face session of School of Researchers I of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, a joint working day in which 140 researchers took part, belonging to the four Schools of Researchers based at the four public universities…
contentEn:
On 4 June, the Faculty of Education in Burgos hosted the final face-to-face session of School of Researchers I of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, a joint working day in which 140 researchers took part, belonging to the four Schools of Researchers based at the four public universities of the region: the University of Burgos, the University of León, the University of Salamanca and the University of Valladolid.

María José Rodríguez Conde, Eva M. Torrecilla and Fernando Martínez Abad, researchers in the Educational Evaluation and Guidance (GE2O) subgroup of the GRIAL research group, attended the event as coordinators of six of the projects presented.

titleEn: Seminar given by Paula Renés Arellano from the University of Cantabria
excerptEn: María José Rodríguez Conde introduced the seminar on «Media education: research into audiovisual competence», given by Paula Renés Arellano, PhD in Education Sciences and lecturer at the University of Cantabria. The seminar took place on Thursday 14 May at the IUCE as part of the calendar of seminars scheduled within the doctoral…
contentEn:
María José Rodríguez Conde introduced the seminar on «Media education: research into audiovisual competence», given by Paula Renés Arellano, PhD in Education Sciences and lecturer at the University of Cantabria. The seminar took place on Thursday 14 May at the IUCE as part of the calendar of seminars scheduled within the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme (http://knowledgesociety.usal.es).

The full seminar can be watched in the following video.

titleEn: New issue of the Revista de Medicina y Cine
excerptEn: Issue 1 of volume 11 of the Revista de Medicina y Cine is now available at http://revistamedicinacine.usal.es Editorial: Teaching and cinema: neurology in Hollywood during 2014 José Elías García Sánchez, Enrique García Sánchez Medicine and Literature: the value of words Josep‐E Baños, Elena Guardiola Original articles: Awake (2007): a real…
contentEn:
Issue 1 of volume 11 of the Revista de Medicina y Cine is now available at http://revistamedicinacine.usal.es

 

 

 

Editorial:

  • Teaching and cinema: neurology in Hollywood during 2014
    José Elías García Sánchez, Enrique García Sánchez
  • Medicine and Literature: the value of words
    Josep‐E Baños, Elena Guardiola

Original articles:

  • Awake (2007): a real nightmare in the operating theatre during a heart transplant
    Juan A. Juanes Méndez
  • Physiology in films: Coma, CO and the understanding of gas exchange
    Josep‐E Baños, Enric Samsó, Magí Farré
  • Beyond empathy: the compassionate gaze in cinema
    Beatriz Ogando Díaz, Eduardo Tejera Torroja
  • Cinema and nursing education
    María Gabriela Felippa, Sandra Livia Mansilla, Rita Sampayo, María José Gabriela Sabelli, Liliana Capul, Silvia Reboredo de Zambonini, Verónica Zaracho
  • Infectious diseases in literature. A long story without an end
    María José Fresnadillo Martínez

Medicine in film stills:

  • Emotional situations at the limit: individual and collective suicide
    Consuelo Lloret Pastor, Andrea Solomando Molina

We have received:

  • On doctors, nurses and illnesses in cinema
    Salvador Rofes, Pedro L. Cano

contentEn: A group of lecturers from the University of Oran (Algeria), led by Professor Bachir Bentahar of the Department of Psychology and Education Sciences, met on Friday 20 March with Mª José Rodríguez Conde, director of the IUCE, in order to prepare a collaboration agreement between the two universities in the areas of research, teaching, and staff and student mobility.

titleEn: Information management: a new challenge for schools

contentEn:
On 11 February, the Diario de Córdoba interviewed María José Rodríguez Conde in connection with the research project «Evaluation, Formation and Innovation on key competences in Secondary Education: ICT, Competence in INformation and Resolution of COnflicts (EFI-CINCO)”, an interdisciplinary project coordinated from the IUCE in which researchers from the universities of Salamanca, Burgos, Cádiz and Córdoba take part.

The full article can be read at http://www.diariocordoba.com/noticias/educacion/gestion-informacion-nuevo-reto-centros-educativos_939078.html.

For more information about the project, visit http://eficinco.usal.es.

titleEn: The IUCE takes part in training for non-university teaching staff within the Regional Government of Castilla y León's “School of Researchers I” innovation project
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca and the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP) are organising, on 29 and 30 January, the initial training session of the educational innovation project “School of Researchers Programme”, now in its second edition. This activity, which…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca and the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP) are organising, on 29 and 30 January, the initial training session of the educational innovation project “School of Researchers Programme”, now in its second edition.

This activity, which will be attended by 30 Pre-school, Primary and Secondary Education teachers from the Salamanca district, is based on the RESOLUTION of 23 October 2014, of the Directorate-General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, establishing the educational innovation project «School of Researchers Programme» for experimenting with new methodologies of continuing teacher training in the field of educational research and innovation during the 2014-15 school year. (BOCyL of 3 November 2014).

This programme has launched the second School of Researchers through three training phases. A face-to-face, theoretical phase, which takes place on 29 and 30 January 2015 and serves as a source of knowledge and an introduction to research, as well as the starting point for the project. In it, different research initiation groups will be set up to address a range of educational problems. An online collaborative phase, which will take place through the CSFP's virtual classroom and will consist of exchange and group learning under the coordination of a member of the IUCE research team. And finally, a practical phase of experimentation, learning and hands-on application in the classroom, to test the link between research and teacher training and teachers' professional development.

The Educational Evaluation and Guidance Group, within the GRIAL group, coordinated by María José Rodríguez Conde, is responsible for monitoring this activity. This academic year, the project is being carried out in parallel at the University of Burgos (coordinated by Raquel Casado's group), at the University of León (coordinated by Mercedes López and her team) and at the University of Valladolid (advised by Bartolomé Rubia's team). The overall group of university advisers is made up of some 20 university lecturers, and the group of enrolled non-university teachers numbers around 120 teachers from Castilla y León.

Photographs: Laura Cardeñosa

titleEn: REDIFOP meeting, 23 January in Salamanca
excerptEn: On 23 January 2015, Salamanca will host the meeting of the Institutional Network for Teacher Training (REDIFOP). The event will run from 9:30 to 14:00 in the Sala de Grados of the Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca of the University of Salamanca. The agenda is as follows: Welcome by…
contentEn:
On 23 January 2015, Salamanca will host the meeting of the Institutional Network for Teacher Training (REDIFOP). The event will run from 9:30 to 14:00 in the Sala de Grados of the Colegio Arzobispo Fonseca of the University of Salamanca.

The agenda is as follows:

  1. Welcome by Ms Carmen Fernández Juncal (Vice-Rector for Teaching of the University of Salamanca) (9:30-10:00)
  2. Presentation and discussion with the expert in university assessment procedures, Mr Eduardo García Jiménez. “Reflections on possible strategies for accrediting university teacher training: possibilities and limitations in the current context” (10:00-11:00)
  3. Coffee break (11:00-11:30)
  4. Presentation of conclusions and discussion on the accreditation of teacher training from the REDU Report: “Proposal for a competence reference framework for university teaching staff and the alignment of training plans based on teaching competences”, by a member of the working team led by Teresa Pagès (ICE-UB) (11:30-12:30)
  5. Open discussion (12:30-14:30)

Reference material for the meeting:

  • European Union: recommendations of the High Level Group on the Modernisation of Higher Education.
  • Final report of the REDU 2012-2014 project: “Proposal for a competence reference framework for university teaching staff and the alignment of training plans based on teaching competences”
  • By way of reminder, the video of the last REDIFOP meeting in Seville (4-12-2013): http://tv.us.es/jornada-redifop-jornada-de-encuentro-de-directores-de-los-ices-y-secretariados-de-formacion-de-las-uiversidades-espanolas/

Follow the live stream at the following link: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8furnfMGfVQ&sns=tw

titleEn: The 19th International Medical Video Competition presents an award to Juan A. Juanes
excerptEn: The joint collaboration between the University Institute of Education Sciences and the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology in Madrid (CITEC-B) has once again borne fruit with the award of the 2014 Videomed Prize, at the 19th International Medical Film Competition, held in Badajoz from 17 to 21 November. The film…
contentEn:
The joint collaboration between the University Institute of Education Sciences and the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology in Madrid (CITEC-B) has once again borne fruit with the award of the 2014 Videomed Prize, at the 19th International Medical Film Competition, held in Badajoz from 17 to 21 November.

The award-winning film is entitled: Gaucher's Disease. This educational audiovisual work aims to offer a concise analysis of this rare congenital autosomal recessive condition, caused by a deficiency of the enzyme glucocerebrosidase. This material provides excellent documentation for a better understanding of the mechanism of lipid storage, which causes bone disorders in the patients who suffer from it, as well as anaemia, thrombocytopenia, cirrhosis and hepatosplenomegaly.

The film presented at the Competition, developed within a technological environment, was praised for its thoroughly innovative design, making it an excellent resource for understanding this medical condition.

There is no doubt that the various audiovisual systems have become a supporting element in the learning of medicine, as well as a vehicle for conveying medical content and an instrument for debate and reflection in medical training.
